3	import "strings"
4	
5	// PushFailedReasonPrefix is the canonical Reason prefix written by the
6	// landing layer when `git push` rejects the new tip after a local merge or
7	// fast-forward succeeded. ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus matches on this prefix
8	// to map a merged-locally-but-not-pushed outcome to push_failed instead of
9	// success, so the bead stays open.
10	const PushFailedReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push failed:"
11	
12	// FailureMode constants classify *why* an execution did not land cleanly.
13	// They are orthogonal to Outcome/Status: a single failed attempt carries one
14	// outcome (e.g. preserved) and one failure mode (e.g. merge_conflict) so
15	// measurement surfaces can aggregate failures by cause.
16	const (
17		FailureModeContextOverflow = "context_overflow"
18		FailureModeMergeConflict   = "merge_conflict"
19		FailureModeTestFailure     = "test_failure"
20		FailureModeBuildFailure    = "build_failure"
21		FailureModeTimeout         = "timeout"
22		FailureModeAuthError       = "auth_error"
23		FailureModeNoChanges       = "no_changes"
24		FailureModeRatchetMiss     = "ratchet_miss"
25		FailureModeUnknown         = "unknown"
26	)
27	
28	// ClassifyFailureMode derives a failure_mode for a worker-level result from
29	// its outcome, exit code, and error message. Returns "" when the outcome is
30	// task_succeeded (success carries no failure mode). task_no_changes always
31	// maps to FailureModeNoChanges regardless of error text.
32	//
33	// For task_failed (or non-zero exit code), the error message is scanned for
34	// well-known substrings. The order matters: context/auth/timeout patterns
35	// are checked before generic test/build patterns so a "test timed out"
36	// classifies as timeout rather than test_failure. Anything unrecognised
37	// maps to FailureModeUnknown so aggregates never hide a missed pattern.
38	func ClassifyFailureMode(outcome string, exitCode int, errMsg string) string {
39		switch outcome {
40		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
41			if exitCode == 0 {
42				return ""
43			}
44			// Degenerate: success outcome with non-zero exit. Treat as unknown
45			// failure rather than success so the aggregate flags the anomaly.
46		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
47			return FailureModeNoChanges
48		}
49	
50		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
51		switch {
52		case containsAny(lower,
53			"context length",
54			"context_length_exceeded",
55			"context window",
56			"maximum context",
57			"prompt is too long",
58			"prompt too long",
59			"token limit",
60			"too many tokens",
61			"exceeds the context"):
62			return FailureModeContextOverflow
63		case containsAny(lower,
64			"unauthorized",
65			"401 unauthorized",
66			"invalid api key",
67			"invalid_api_key",
68			"authentication failed",
69			"authentication_error",
70			"no api key",
71			"missing api key",
72			"quota exceeded",
73			"insufficient quota",
74			"insufficient_quota",
75			"rate limit",
76			"rate_limit",
77			"429"):
78			return FailureModeAuthError
79		case containsAny(lower,
80			"timeout",
81			"timed out",
82			"context deadline exceeded",
83			"deadline exceeded",
84			"signal: killed"):
85			return FailureModeTimeout
86		case containsAny(lower,
87			"merge conflict",
88			"ff-merge not possible",
89			"merge failed",
90			"automatic merge failed"):
91			return FailureModeMergeConflict
92		case containsAny(lower,
93			"build failed",
94			"compilation failed",
95			"compile error",
96			"compilation error",
97			"cannot find package",
98			"undefined:",
99			"undefined reference",
100			"syntax error"):
101			return FailureModeBuildFailure
102		case containsAny(lower,
103			"test failed",
104			"tests failed",
105			"--- fail:",
106			"assertion failed",
107			"test failure"):
108			return FailureModeTestFailure
109		}
110		if exitCode != 0 {
111			return FailureModeUnknown
112		}
113		return ""
114	}
115	
116	// containsAny reports whether s contains any of the given substrings. s is
117	// assumed to already be lower-cased by the caller.
118	func containsAny(s string, needles ...string) bool {
119		for _, n := range needles {
120			if n == "" {
121				continue
122			}
123			if strings.Contains(s, n) {
124				return true
125			}
126		}
127		return false
128	}
129	
130	// classifyLandingFailureMode computes the final failure_mode for a result
131	// after the orchestrator has made its landing decision. It prefers
132	// landing-specific signals (merge conflict, gate failure) over the worker's
133	// classification and falls back to workerMode for worker-level failures
134	// (agent execution failed, context overflow, etc.). Returns "" when the
135	// bead merged cleanly.
136	func classifyLandingFailureMode(landingOutcome, landingReason string, gateResults []GateCheckResult, workerMode string) string {
137		switch landingOutcome {
138		case "merged":
139			return ""
140		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
141			return FailureModeNoChanges
142		case "error":
143			if workerMode != "" {
144				return workerMode
145			}
146			return FailureModeUnknown
147		case "preserved":
148			switch landingReason {
149			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
150				return FailureModeMergeConflict
151			case "post-run checks failed":
152				return classifyGateFailure(gateResults)
153			case RatchetPreserveReason:
154				return FailureModeRatchetMiss
155			}
156			// Other preserved reasons (e.g. "agent execution failed",
157			// "--no-merge specified") defer to the worker's classification.
158			if workerMode != "" {
159				return workerMode
160			}
161			return FailureModeUnknown
162		}
163		if workerMode != "" {
164			return workerMode
165		}
166		return FailureModeUnknown
167	}
168	
169	// classifyGateFailure inspects failed gate results to distinguish
170	// build_failure from test_failure. Gate definitions that fail to compile
171	// before running (go build, tsc, cargo build) typically surface build
172	// diagnostics in stderr; test runners surface test failure markers. When
173	// ambiguous, defaults to test_failure since the post-run checks gate is
174	// test-oriented by convention.
175	func classifyGateFailure(gateResults []GateCheckResult) string {
176		for _, gr := range gateResults {
177			if gr.Status != "fail" {
178				continue
179			}
180			combined := strings.ToLower(gr.Stdout + "\n" + gr.Stderr)
181			if containsAny(combined,
182				"build failed",
183				"compilation failed",
184				"compile error",
185				"compilation error",
186				"cannot find package",
187				"undefined:",
188				"undefined reference",
189				"syntax error") {
190				return FailureModeBuildFailure
191			}
192		}
193		return FailureModeTestFailure
194	}
195	
196	// Worker-level outcome constants — set by ExecuteBead() on the result.
197	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult) then
198	// overwrites Outcome and Status with the landing decision.
199	const (
200		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded = "task_succeeded"
201		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed    = "task_failed"
202		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges = "task_no_changes"
203	)
204	
205	// Status constants — used by the loop and other consumers of ExecuteBeadReport.
206	// These map to specific close/unclaim behaviors in ExecuteBeadWorker.
207	const (
208		ExecuteBeadStatusStructuralValidationFailed = "structural_validation_failed"
209		ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed            = "execution_failed"
210		ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed         = "post_run_check_failed"
211		ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed              = "ratchet_failed"
212		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict               = "land_conflict"
213		ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed                 = "push_failed"
214		ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"
215		ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied           = "already_satisfied"
216		ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess                    = "success"
217	
218		// ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition is a structured outcome
219		// signalling that the executor inspected the bead and concluded it cannot
220		// be delivered in a single pass — typically because the scope is
221		// epic-sized and must first be broken into sub-beads. It is distinct
222		// from no_changes (which means "I tried and there was nothing to change")
223		// and from execution_failed (which means the agent or harness errored).
224		//
225		// The loop treats this status as terminal-for-loop: it parks the bead
226		// with a long cooldown and records the recommended sub-beads as a
227		// structured `decomposition-recommendation` event. No further attempts
228		// happen until an operator clears the cooldown (e.g. via
229		// `ddx bead cooldown clear`) or splits the bead.
230		ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition = "declined_needs_decomposition"
231	
232		// Post-merge review outcomes. The bead was merged, then reviewed;
233		// the review returned a non-APPROVE verdict and the bead was reopened.
234		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges = "review_request_changes"
235		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock          = "review_block"
236		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction    = "review_malfunction"
237	)
238	
239	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
240	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
241	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
242	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
243		if exitCode != 0 {
244			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
245		}
246	
247		switch outcome {
248		case "merged":
249			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
250			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
251			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
252			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
253			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
254			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {
255				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
256			}
257			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
258		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
259			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
260		case "error":
261			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
262		case "preserved":
263			switch reason {
264			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
265				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
266			case "post-run checks failed":
267				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
268			case RatchetPreserveReason:
269				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
270			default:
271				// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
272				// explicitly requested no merge.
273				return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
274			}
275		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
276			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
277		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
278			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
279		default:
280			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
281		}
282	}
283	
284	func ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(status, reason, errMsg string) string {
285		switch {
286		case reason != "" && errMsg != "" && reason != errMsg:
287			return reason + ": " + errMsg
288		case reason != "":
289			return reason
290		case errMsg != "":
291			return errMsg
292		case status != "":
293			return status
294		default:
295			return "unknown"
296		}
297	}

14	// TestClassifyExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed pins the contract that a merged
15	// outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker classifies as
16	// push_failed (not success), so the execute-loop refuses to close the bead.
17	func TestClassifyExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed(t *testing.T) {
18		got := ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus("merged", 0, PushFailedReasonPrefix+" remote rejected: pre-receive hook declined")
19		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed, got,
20			"merged outcome with push-failed reason must classify as push_failed, not success")
21	
22		gotMerged := ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus("merged", 0, "merged onto current tip")
23		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, gotMerged,
24			"merged outcome without push-failed reason must still classify as success")
25	}
26	
27	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerPushFailedStaysOpenAndParks asserts the AC for
28	// ddx-af54ebf3:
29	//   - bead is NOT closed (status remains open)
30	//   - last_status=push_failed and the push stderr are stored as cooldown metadata
31	//   - the bead's events surface the push stderr so an operator can see it
32	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerPushFailedStaysOpenAndParks(t *testing.T) {
33		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
34		pushStderr := "remote: error: GH001: large files detected; aborting"
35		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
36			Store: store,
37			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
38				return ExecuteBeadReport{
39					BeadID:    beadID,
40					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed,
41					Detail:    PushFailedReasonPrefix + " " + pushStderr,
42					SessionID: "sess-push-failed",
43					BaseRev:   "aaaa1111",
44					ResultRev: "bbbb2222",
45				}, nil
46			}),
47		}
48	
49		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
50		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
51		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
52		require.NoError(t, err)
53		require.NotNil(t, result)
54		assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, result.Failures, 1)
55		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed, result.LastFailureStatus)
56	
57		got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
58		require.NoError(t, err)
59		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status, "push_failed must NOT close the bead")
60		assert.Empty(t, got.Owner, "push_failed must release the claim")
61	
62		require.NotNil(t, got.Extra)
63		assert.Equal(t, "push_failed", got.Extra["execute-loop-last-status"],
64			"loop must record last_status=push_failed so subsequent claim attempts can refuse")
65		detail, _ := got.Extra["execute-loop-last-detail"].(string)
66		assert.Contains(t, detail, pushStderr,
67			"loop must record the push stderr in last_detail so operators can see why")
68		assert.NotEmpty(t, got.Extra["execute-loop-retry-after"],
69			"push_failed must park the bead via execute-loop-retry-after")
70	
71		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
72		require.NoError(t, err)
73		require.NotEmpty(t, events)
74		var sawPushStderr bool
75		for _, ev := range events {
76			if ev.Summary == ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed && strings.Contains(ev.Body, pushStderr) {
77				sawPushStderr = true
78				break
79			}
80		}
81		assert.True(t, sawPushStderr,
82			"push stderr must appear in a bead event so operators can see why the push failed")
83	}
84	
85	// TestClaimRefusesPushFailedBead pins the AC that subsequent claim attempts
86	// on a push-failed bead fail loudly until the operator clears
87	// execute-loop-last-status.
88	func TestClaimRefusesPushFailedBead(t *testing.T) {
89		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
90		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
91	
92		b := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-pf01", Title: "push-failed", Priority: 0}
93		require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
94	
95		require.NoError(t, store.Update(b.ID, func(bb *bead.Bead) {
96			if bb.Extra == nil {
97				bb.Extra = map[string]any{}
98			}
99			bb.Extra["execute-loop-last-status"] = "push_failed"
100			bb.Extra["execute-loop-last-detail"] = PushFailedReasonPrefix + " remote: GH001: large files detected"
101		}))
102	
103		err := store.Claim(b.ID, "worker")
104		require.Error(t, err, "Claim must refuse a bead whose last_status=push_failed")
105		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "push failed")
106		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "GH001",
107			"the previous push stderr must surface in the claim error so it fails loudly")
108	
109		// Clearing the last_status restores claimability.
110		require.NoError(t, store.Update(b.ID, func(bb *bead.Bead) {
111			delete(bb.Extra, "execute-loop-last-status")
112			delete(bb.Extra, "execute-loop-last-detail")
113		}))
114		require.NoError(t, store.Claim(b.ID, "worker"),
115			"Claim must succeed after operator clears execute-loop-last-status")
116	}

3	// execute_bead_land.go — the land coordinator pattern.
4	//
5	// This file implements the human-PR landing model for execute-bead results.
6	// The old Merge() path in execute_bead_orchestrator.go has been deleted; all
7	// target-ref writes now flow through Land(), which is called exactly once per
8	// submission by a per-project coordinator goroutine (see
9	// internal/server/workers.go:LandCoordinator).
10	//
11	// The flow mirrors how a human merges PRs:
12	//
13	//   1. Fetch the current target tip (from origin when a remote exists, or
14	//      from the local branch otherwise).
15	//   2. If the current tip equals the worker's BaseRev — fast-forward the
16	//      target branch directly to the worker's ResultRev via update-ref. The
17	//      worker's commit keeps its original parent; no new commit is created.
18	//   3. Otherwise — the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
19	//      a temporary detached worktree at the current target tip, run
20	//      `git merge --no-ff` to introduce the worker's ResultRev, and
21	//      fast-forward the target branch to the resulting merge commit. The
22	//      worker's commit is NEVER rewritten: its parent is still BaseRev, so
23	//      a later replay observes the same inputs the worker saw.
24	//   4. If the merge conflicts — abort cleanly, preserve the original
25	//      ResultRev under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>,
26	//      and return preserved status. Target branch is never modified.
27	//   5. If an origin remote exists — push the new target tip. The push is
28	//      strictly fast-forward; push failures are reported via PushFailed but
29	//      do not roll back the local target ref.
30	//
31	// Replay fidelity is the reason for merge-over-rebase: a rebased commit has
32	// a rewritten parent that lies about what the worker saw at execution time.
33	// A merge commit preserves both histories — the worker's original commit
34	// (parent = BaseRev) and the target's prior tip — losslessly.
35	//
36	// The coordinator owning the goroutine provides the serialization guarantee,
37	// so Land() itself does not take any locks.
38	
39	import (
40		"context"
41		"fmt"
42		"os"
43		"path/filepath"
44		"strings"

46		internalgit "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
47	)

49	// LandRequest is one submission to the land coordinator: "here is the worker's
50	// result from base B to result R for bead X; land it on the project's target
51	// branch."
52	type LandRequest struct {
53		// WorktreeDir is the path to the project's repository directory (the
54		// directory the coordinator operates on). The original worker worktree
55		// has typically already been removed by the time Land() runs — Land()
56		// creates its own temporary worktrees when a merge is needed.
57		WorktreeDir string
58	
59		// BaseRev is the revision the worker branched off when it started.
60		// When the current target tip equals BaseRev, Land() takes the fast path.
61		BaseRev string
62	
63		// ResultRev is the worker's final commit SHA. Must be reachable in the
64		// project's git object store at the time Land() is called.
65		ResultRev string
66	
67		// BeadID identifies the bead this submission is for. Used to build the
68		// preserve-ref path on conflict.
69		BeadID string
70	
71		// AttemptID uniquely identifies this land attempt. Used to build the
72		// preserve-ref path on conflict so concurrent attempts for the same
73		// bead do not collide.
74		AttemptID string
75	
76		// TargetBranch is the branch to advance. When empty, Land() resolves
77		// the project's current HEAD branch and uses that.
78		TargetBranch string
79	
80		// EvidenceDir is the relative path to the per-attempt execution evidence
81		// directory (e.g. ".ddx/executions/20260416T181205-b5419982"). When
82		// non-empty and the main land succeeds, Land() creates a trailing
83		// evidence commit that folds these files into the target branch. The
84		// agent's commit SHA is NOT amended — the evidence commit is a separate
85		// child commit, preserving closing_commit_sha references.
86		EvidenceDir string
87	}

89	// LandResult describes the outcome of a single Land() call.
90	type LandResult struct {
91		// Status is one of:
92		//   - "landed":    the target branch now points at a new commit
93		//                  (either ResultRev itself on the fast-forward path,
94		//                  or a merge commit on the merge path).
95		//   - "preserved": the merge conflicted; ResultRev is saved under
96		//                  PreserveRef and the target branch is unchanged.
97		//   - "no-changes": ResultRev == BaseRev; nothing to land.
98		Status string
99	
100		// NewTip is the new value of the target branch when Status == "landed".
101		// On the fast-forward path NewTip == ResultRev; on the merge path NewTip
102		// is the SHA of the merge commit (whose parents are the prior target
103		// tip and ResultRev). Empty when preserved or no-changes.
104		NewTip string
105	
106		// Merged is true when the land took the merge-commit path (current tip
107		// had advanced beyond BaseRev, so Land() created a merge commit to
108		// combine the worker's result with the new target tip). False on the
109		// fast-forward path where the worker's commit became the new tip
110		// unchanged.
111		Merged bool
112	
113		// PreserveRef is set when Status == "preserved". It names the ref under
114		// refs/ddx/iterations/ where ResultRev was saved for later recovery.
115		PreserveRef string
116	
117		// Reason is a human-readable explanation, especially useful when
118		// Status == "preserved" (e.g. "merge conflict") or when PushFailed.
119		Reason string
120	
121		// PushFailed is true when the local target ref was advanced successfully
122		// but the subsequent push to origin was rejected (e.g. non-fast-forward).
123		// The local state is authoritative; the remote will need to be
124		// reconciled by a later land or an operator.
125		PushFailed bool
126	
127		// PushError captures the underlying push error when PushFailed is true.
128		PushError string
129	
130		// MergedCommitCount is the number of commits reachable from ResultRev but
131		// not from BaseRev — i.e. the "size" of the worker's contribution being
132		// merged in. Zero on the no-changes path. Set on both the fast-forward
133		// and merge-commit paths so metrics can compare contribution sizes.
134		MergedCommitCount int
135	
136		// EvidenceCommitSHA is set when a trailing execution-evidence commit was
137		// created after the main land. When set, NewTip points at this commit
138		// (not at the original agent commit or merge commit).
139		EvidenceCommitSHA string
140	}

142	// PreClaimResult is the outcome of a FetchOriginAncestryCheck call.
143	type PreClaimResult struct {
144		// Action is one of:
145		//   "unchanged"     — local tip == origin tip, no action taken
146		//   "fast-forwarded"— local was behind origin; local branch was advanced
147		//   "no-origin"     — no origin remote; check skipped
148		//   "local-ahead"   — local is ahead of origin; no action needed
149		//   "diverged"      — neither is ancestor of the other; claim should be aborted
150		Action    string
151		LocalSHA  string
152		OriginSHA string
153	}

157	type LandingGitOps interface {

175		UpdateRefTo(dir, ref, sha, oldSHA string) error
176	
177		// SyncWorkTreeToHead syncs the index AND the working-tree files in dir
178		// to HEAD after a non-checkout ref update (e.g. update-ref). fromRev is
179		// the commit HEAD pointed at BEFORE the ref update; it is used to
180		// compute the minimal set of tracked files changed by the update so
181		// that unrelated local modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat
182		// writes, operator scratch) are NOT clobbered.
183		//
184		// Needed because Land() advances the target ref via update-ref, which
185		// touches neither the index nor the worktree. Before this fix, Land()
186		// only ran `git read-tree HEAD` to sync the index — leaving the main
187		// worktree showing phantom deleted/modified entries for every file the
188		// landed commit touched, and subsequent agents reading files from disk
189		// would see stale content.
190		//
191		// Implementation: `git read-tree HEAD` + `git diff --name-only fromRev
192		// HEAD` to enumerate changed files + `git checkout-index -f --` to
193		// materialize them from the freshly-synced index.
194		SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error
195	
196		// AddWorktree creates a new worktree at path checked out at rev in dir.
197		AddWorktree(dir, path, rev string) error
198	
199		// RemoveWorktree removes the worktree at path in dir (force).
200		RemoveWorktree(dir, path string) error
201	
202		// MergeInto runs `git merge --no-ff -m msg srcRev` inside wtDir, which
203		// must already be checked out at the current target tip. A clean merge
204		// produces a merge commit whose parents are [currentTip, srcRev]; the
205		// commit SHA can be read with HeadRevAt. Returns nil on clean merge,
206		// or an error on conflict. On error, the implementation is responsible
207		// for running `git merge --abort` so the worktree is left clean.
208		MergeInto(wtDir, srcRev, msg string) error
209	
210		// HeadRevAt returns HEAD of the git workdir at dir.
211		HeadRevAt(dir string) (string, error)
212	
213		// PushFFOnly pushes localRef to remote as targetBranch with strict
214		// fast-forward semantics. Returns an error when the push would not be
215		// fast-forward or when the network call fails.
216		PushFFOnly(dir, remote, localRef, targetBranch string) error
217	
218		// FetchOriginAncestryCheck fetches origin/targetBranch and compares it
219		// to the local branch tip. When origin is ahead the local branch is
220		// fast-forwarded via update-ref + read-tree. When the two tips have
221		// diverged (neither is ancestor of the other) the returned result has
222		// Action == "diverged". When no origin remote exists the result has
223		// Action == "no-origin". The caller decides whether to abort a claim.
224		FetchOriginAncestryCheck(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error)
225	
226		// CountCommits returns the number of commits reachable from tip but not
227		// from base (i.e. git rev-list --count base..tip). Used to record the
228		// contribution size in land metrics. Returns 0 on error.
229		CountCommits(dir, base, tip string) int
230	
231		// StageDir stages all files under relPath in dir for the next commit.
232		StageDir(dir, relPath string) error
233	
234		// CommitStaged creates a commit from currently staged changes using msg
235		// as the commit message. Returns (sha, nil) when a commit was made,
236		// ("", nil) when nothing was staged, and ("", err) on failure.
237		CommitStaged(dir, msg string) (string, error)
238	}

292	func (RealLandingGitOps) SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error {
293		// Step 1: sync the index to HEAD. This is required before checkout-index
294		// below will do anything useful, and also keeps subsequent CommitTracker
295		// calls from building stale trees.
296		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "read-tree", "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
297		if err != nil {
298			return fmt.Errorf("git read-tree HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
299		}
300	
301		// Step 2: compute the list of tracked files changed between the previous
302		// HEAD and the current HEAD. These are the files that the landing commit
303		// added, modified, or deleted. We only restore THESE files to avoid
304		// clobbering unrelated local modifications (agent-logs being written by
305		// the running server, beads.jsonl heartbeat updates, operator scratch).
306		if fromRev == "" {
307			// No previous HEAD known — fall back to the unsafe behaviour of
308			// read-tree only. Acceptable when the caller is a best-effort path
309			// that cannot reconstruct fromRev.
310			return nil
311		}
312		diffOut, diffErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--name-only", fromRev, "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
313		if diffErr != nil {
314			// Diff failed (bad fromRev, shallow history, etc.) — leave the
315			// worktree stale rather than risk a broken checkout. The CommitTracker
316			// stale-tree bug is the prior status quo and no worse than before.
317			return nil
318		}
319		changed := strings.Fields(strings.TrimSpace(string(diffOut)))
320		if len(changed) == 0 {
321			return nil
322		}
323	
324		// Step 3: split into existing-in-HEAD (checkout-index) and
325		// deleted-in-HEAD (os.Remove) buckets. checkout-index only writes files
326		// that are in the index; it cannot represent a deletion, so we handle
327		// those ourselves.
328		var indexFiles []string
329		var removedFiles []string
330		for _, f := range changed {
331			probe := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "ls-files", "--error-unmatch", "--", f)
332			if probe.Run() == nil {
333				indexFiles = append(indexFiles, f)
334			} else {
335				removedFiles = append(removedFiles, f)
336			}
337		}
338	
339		// Step 4: materialize the index-present files into the working tree.
340		// -f overwrites any stale content at these exact paths. Unrelated files
341		// are untouched because we pass the specific path list.
342		if len(indexFiles) > 0 {
343			args := []string{"checkout-index", "-f", "--"}
344			args = append(args, indexFiles...)
345			out2, err2 := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).CombinedOutput()
346			if err2 != nil {
347				return fmt.Errorf("git checkout-index -f: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out2)), err2)
348			}
349		}
350	
351		// Step 5: remove files that the landing commit deleted and whose removal
352		// did not propagate to the worktree because update-ref bypassed the
353		// working-tree update.
354		for _, f := range removedFiles {
355			full := filepath.Join(dir, f)
356			_ = os.Remove(full) // best-effort; leave the file if removal fails
357		}
358	
359		return nil
360	}

365		if err != nil {
366			return fmt.Errorf("git worktree add %s %s: %s: %w", path, rev, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)

377	func (RealLandingGitOps) MergeInto(wtDir, srcRev, msg string) error {
378		// --no-ff forces a merge commit even when the merge could fast-forward
379		// (which shouldn't happen given our caller's ancestry check, but is a
380		// defensive guarantee that target history always gets a marker commit).
381		// We inject user.name/user.email via -c so the merge commit can be
382		// created even when the worktree inherited no git config; the
383		// coordinator is a machine actor and should not adopt a human's identity.
384		out, err := internalgit.Command(
385			context.Background(), wtDir,
386			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
387			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
388			"merge", "--no-ff", "-m", msg, srcRev,
389		).CombinedOutput()
390		if err != nil {
391			_ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wtDir, "merge", "--abort").Run()
392			return fmt.Errorf("git merge --no-ff %s: %s: %w", srcRev, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
393		}
394		return nil
395	}

415	func (RealLandingGitOps) CountCommits(dir, base, tip string) int {
416		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-list", "--count", base+".."+tip).CombinedOutput()
417		if err != nil {
418			return 0
419		}
420		s := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
421		n := 0
422		for _, c := range s {
423			if c >= '0' && c <= '9' {
424				n = n*10 + int(c-'0')

426		}
427		return n
428	}

447		if len(strings.TrimSpace(string(out))) == 0 {
448			return "", nil
449		}
450		commitOut, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir,
451			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",

453			"commit", "-m", msg,
454		).CombinedOutput()

457		}

459		if err != nil {
460			return "", fmt.Errorf("rev-parse HEAD after evidence commit: %w", err)
461		}

465	// FetchOriginAncestryCheck implements LandingGitOps.FetchOriginAncestryCheck.
466	func (RealLandingGitOps) FetchOriginAncestryCheck(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error) {
467		// Step 1: check for origin remote.
468		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "remote", "get-url", "origin").CombinedOutput()
469		if err != nil {
470			// No origin remote — single-machine case; skip check.
471			return PreClaimResult{Action: "no-origin"}, nil
472		}
473		_ = out // remote URL not needed
474	
475		// Step 2: fetch origin/targetBranch (best-effort; failure is non-fatal but surfaced).
476		if fetchOut, fetchErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "fetch", "origin", targetBranch).CombinedOutput(); fetchErr != nil {
477			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("git fetch origin %s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(fetchOut)), fetchErr)
478		}
479	
480		// Step 3: resolve local tip.
481		localOut, localErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/heads/"+targetBranch).CombinedOutput()
482		if localErr != nil {
483			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolving local %s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(localOut)), localErr)
484		}
485		localSHA := strings.TrimSpace(string(localOut))
486	
487		// Step 4: resolve origin tip.
488		originOut, originErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/remotes/origin/"+targetBranch).CombinedOutput()
489		if originErr != nil {
490			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolving origin/%s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(originOut)), originErr)
491		}
492		originSHA := strings.TrimSpace(string(originOut))
493	
494		// Step 5: compare.
495		if localSHA == originSHA {
496			return PreClaimResult{Action: "unchanged", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
497		}
498	
499		// Is local an ancestor of origin? (origin is ahead)
500		localAncestorErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", localSHA, originSHA).Run()
501		if localAncestorErr == nil {
502			// Origin is ahead: fast-forward local branch via update-ref + sync worktree.
503			targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
504			if upErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", targetRef, originSHA, localSHA).Run(); upErr != nil {
505				return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to %s: %w", targetRef, originSHA, upErr)
506			}
507			// Sync index + working tree to new HEAD so the main worktree files
508			// reflect the pulled-down origin commits. Pass localSHA as fromRev
509			// to restrict the restore to files actually changed by origin's
510			// advance, preserving unrelated local modifications.
511			_ = (RealLandingGitOps{}).SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, localSHA)
512			return PreClaimResult{Action: "fast-forwarded", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
513		}
514	
515		// Is origin an ancestor of local? (local is ahead)
516		originAncestorErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", originSHA, localSHA).Run()
517		if originAncestorErr == nil {
518			return PreClaimResult{Action: "local-ahead", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
519		}
520	
521		// Neither is ancestor of the other: diverged.
522		return PreClaimResult{Action: "diverged", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
523	}
524	
525	// landIterationRef returns the documented hidden ref for a land-time preserve.
526	// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>. The short-tip
527	// captures the current target tip at the time of the conflict so subsequent
528	// recovery tools can reconstruct which merge target was in play.
529	func landIterationRef(beadID, attemptID, tip string) string {
530		short := tip
531		if len(short) > 12 {
532			short = short[:12]
533		}
534		attempt := attemptID
535		if attempt == "" {
536			attempt = NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
537		}
538		return fmt.Sprintf("refs/ddx/iterations/%s/%s-%s", beadID, attempt, short)
539	}
540	
541	// landEvidence creates a trailing commit that folds the per-attempt execution
542	// evidence directory into the target branch. Called after the main land (ff or
543	// merge) succeeds and before the push. The evidence commit is a normal child of
544	// result.NewTip — the agent's original commit SHA is not amended.
545	//
546	// Best-effort: only works when HEAD is a symbolic ref to targetBranch (the
547	// normal case for server workers and CLI users). When HEAD is detached or
548	// points elsewhere, the evidence commit is silently skipped.
549	func landEvidence(wd, targetBranch string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps, result *LandResult) {
550		branch, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
551		if err != nil || branch != targetBranch {
552			return
553		}
554		if err := gitOps.StageDir(wd, req.EvidenceDir); err != nil {
555			return
556		}
557		msg := fmt.Sprintf("chore: add execution evidence [%s]", shortAttempt(req.AttemptID))
558		sha, err := gitOps.CommitStaged(wd, msg)
559		if err != nil || sha == "" {
560			return
561		}
562		result.EvidenceCommitSHA = sha
563		result.NewTip = sha
564	}
565	
566	// Land performs fetch → (ff or merge) → push for a single submission.
567	// Callers MUST serialize calls per projectRoot (the server coordinator
568	// goroutine provides this). Land() itself takes no internal locks.
569	//
570	// projectRoot is the directory containing the project's .git. req.WorktreeDir,
571	// when non-empty, is used as the git working directory for all commands; when
572	// empty, projectRoot is used. Both usually refer to the same dir — the field
573	// is kept for forward compatibility with multi-clone topologies.
574	func Land(projectRoot string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps) (*LandResult, error) {
575		if gitOps == nil {
576			gitOps = RealLandingGitOps{}
577		}
578		wd := req.WorktreeDir
579		if wd == "" {
580			wd = projectRoot
581		}
582	
583		// Trivial guard — no commits to land.
584		if req.ResultRev == "" || req.ResultRev == req.BaseRev {
585			return &LandResult{Status: "no-changes", Reason: "result_rev == base_rev"}, nil
586		}
587	
588		// Resolve target branch (default to project HEAD).
589		targetBranch := req.TargetBranch
590		if targetBranch == "" {
591			br, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
592			if err != nil {
593				return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving target branch: %w", err)
594			}
595			targetBranch = br
596		}
597		targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
598	
599		// Fetch the current target tip from origin when available; otherwise
600		// read the local branch directly.
601		hasOrigin := gitOps.HasRemote(wd, "origin")
602		if hasOrigin {
603			// Fetch is best-effort: a disconnected remote must not block
604			// a local land. Log-style errors are swallowed here; the
605			// subsequent ResolveRef will surface any fatal issue.
606			_ = gitOps.FetchBranch(wd, "origin", targetBranch)
607		}
608		currentTip, err := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, targetRef)
609		if err != nil {
610			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving target tip %s: %w", targetRef, err)
611		}
612	
613		contribCount := gitOps.CountCommits(wd, req.BaseRev, req.ResultRev)
614	
615		// Fast path: no sibling advanced the target branch → straight ff via
616		// update-ref. The worker's commit becomes the new tip unchanged; its
617		// parent is still BaseRev, so replay sees the same inputs the worker
618		// saw. No merge commit is created.
619		if currentTip == req.BaseRev {
620			if err := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, req.ResultRev, currentTip); err != nil {
621				return nil, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to %s: %w", targetRef, req.ResultRev, err)
622			}
623			// Refresh the main worktree's index + working-tree files so the
624			// operator and subsequent agents see the newly-landed commit's
625			// changes. Pass currentTip as fromRev so only files actually
626			// changed by this commit are restored — unrelated local
627			// modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat) are preserved.
628			// Non-fatal on error.
629			_ = gitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead(wd, currentTip)
630			result := &LandResult{
631				Status:            "landed",
632				NewTip:            req.ResultRev,
633				Merged:            false,
634				MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
635			}
636			if req.EvidenceDir != "" {
637				landEvidence(wd, targetBranch, req, gitOps, result)
638			}
639			landPush(wd, targetBranch, result.NewTip, hasOrigin, gitOps, result)
640			return result, nil
641		}
642	
643		// Merge path: the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
644		// a temp detached worktree at currentTip and run `git merge --no-ff
645		// ResultRev` inside it. The result is a merge commit whose parents are
646		// [currentTip, ResultRev]. Crucially, ResultRev itself is NOT rewritten:
647		// its parent is still BaseRev, so replay observes the original inputs.
648		tempWT, tempWtErr := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-land-wt-*")
649		if tempWtErr != nil {
650			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating temp worktree dir: %w", tempWtErr)
651		}
652		// os.MkdirTemp creates the dir, but git worktree add refuses to run if
653		// the target already exists. Remove it first so git can recreate it.
654		_ = os.RemoveAll(tempWT)
655		if err := gitOps.AddWorktree(wd, tempWT, currentTip); err != nil {
656			return nil, fmt.Errorf("adding temp worktree at %s: %w", currentTip, err)
657		}
658		defer func() { _ = gitOps.RemoveWorktree(wd, tempWT) }()
659	
660		mergeMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Merge bead %s attempt %s into %s", req.BeadID, shortAttempt(req.AttemptID), targetBranch)
661		if err := gitOps.MergeInto(tempWT, req.ResultRev, mergeMsg); err != nil {
662			// Merge conflict: preserve the original ResultRev and return.
663			preserveRef := landIterationRef(req.BeadID, req.AttemptID, currentTip)
664			if upErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, preserveRef, req.ResultRev, ""); upErr != nil {
665				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving %s after merge conflict: %w", preserveRef, upErr)
666			}
667			return &LandResult{
668				Status:            "preserved",
669				PreserveRef:       preserveRef,
670				Reason:            "merge conflict",
671				MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
672			}, nil
673		}
674	
675		// Merge clean: read the merge commit SHA from the temp worktree's HEAD
676		// and fast-forward the target branch to it.
677		mergeSHA, err := gitOps.HeadRevAt(tempWT)
678		if err != nil {
679			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading merge HEAD: %w", err)
680		}
681		if err := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, mergeSHA, currentTip); err != nil {
682			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to merge commit %s: %w", targetRef, mergeSHA, err)
683		}
684		// Refresh the main worktree's index + working-tree files. Pass
685		// currentTip as fromRev; the merge commit's tree contains both the
686		// current-tip files and the incoming ResultRev files, so the diff
687		// against currentTip yields exactly the files affected by the merge.
688		// Unrelated local modifications are preserved.
689		_ = gitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead(wd, currentTip)
690	
691		result := &LandResult{
692			Status:            "landed",
693			NewTip:            mergeSHA,
694			Merged:            true,
695			MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
696		}
697		if req.EvidenceDir != "" {
698			landEvidence(wd, targetBranch, req, gitOps, result)
699		}
700		landPush(wd, targetBranch, result.NewTip, hasOrigin, gitOps, result)
701		return result, nil
702	}
703	
704	// landPush pushes the new target tip to origin when a remote exists. Push
705	// failure is non-fatal for the local land outcome; it is surfaced via
706	// PushFailed/PushError on the result.
707	func landPush(wd, targetBranch, newTip string, hasOrigin bool, gitOps LandingGitOps, result *LandResult) {
708		if !hasOrigin {
709			return
710		}
711		if err := gitOps.PushFFOnly(wd, "origin", newTip, targetBranch); err != nil {
712			result.PushFailed = true
713			result.PushError = err.Error()
714		}
715	}
716	
717	// shortAttempt returns a 10-char slug derived from attemptID for use in temp
718	// branch names. When attemptID is empty, it returns the current timestamp.
719	func shortAttempt(attemptID string) string {
720		if attemptID != "" {
721			if len(attemptID) > 16 {
722				return attemptID[:16]
723			}
724			return attemptID
725		}
726		return NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405")
727	}
728	
729	// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult maps a LandResult onto an
730	// ExecuteBeadResult so the execute-bead loop sees the correct final status.
731	// It is the coordinator-pattern replacement for ApplyLandingToResult.
732	func ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult(res *ExecuteBeadResult, land *LandResult) {
733		if land == nil || res == nil {
734			return
735		}
736		switch land.Status {
737		case "landed":
738			// Fast-forward or merge commit — either way the target branch now
739			// contains the worker's result. ResultRev's own parent is still
740			// BaseRev so replay fidelity is preserved.
741			res.Outcome = "merged"
742			res.Reason = ""
743			res.PreserveRef = ""
744			if land.Merged {
745				res.Reason = "merged onto current tip"
746			}
747			if land.PushFailed {
748				res.Reason = PushFailedReasonPrefix + " " + land.PushError
749			}
750			// NewTip reflects the ref actually on the target branch (either
751			// ResultRev on the ff path or the merge commit SHA on the merge path).
752			if land.NewTip != "" {
753				res.ResultRev = land.NewTip
754			}
755		case "preserved":
756			res.Outcome = "preserved"
757			res.Reason = land.Reason
758			res.PreserveRef = land.PreserveRef
759		case "no-changes":
760			// Only overwrite when the worker itself did not already report
761			// a richer no-changes rationale.
762			if res.Outcome == "" || res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded {
763				res.Outcome = "no-changes"
764			}
765			if res.Reason == "" {
766				res.Reason = land.Reason
767			}
768		}
769		// Re-classify loop-visible status from the landing outcome.
770		reasonForStatus := res.Reason
771		if land.Status == "preserved" {
772			// Route preserve reasons through the land-conflict classifier so the
773			// loop sees land_conflict (not generic success).
774			reasonForStatus = "merge conflict"
775		}
776		res.Status = ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, reasonForStatus)
777		res.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, res.Reason, res.Error)
778	}
779	
780	// BuildLandRequestFromResult constructs a LandRequest for the coordinator from
781	// an ExecuteBeadResult. The coordinator always passes projectRoot as the
782	// workdir — the worker's original worktree has already been cleaned up by the
783	// time Land() runs.
784	func BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult) LandRequest {
785		return LandRequest{
786			WorktreeDir:  projectRoot,
787			BaseRev:      res.BaseRev,
788			ResultRev:    res.ResultRev,
789			BeadID:       res.BeadID,
790			AttemptID:    res.AttemptID,
791			TargetBranch: "",
792			EvidenceDir:  res.ExecutionDir,
793		}
794	}

3	// execute_bead_land_test.go — Land() coordinator-pattern unit tests.
4	//
5	// These tests run against a real temp git repo so they exercise the actual
6	// git plumbing (update-ref, merge --no-ff, worktree add, etc.) rather than a
7	// mock. Each scenario asserts that the target tip advances correctly and —
8	// crucially for replay fidelity — that the worker's own commit is never
9	// rewritten. Its parent always stays BaseRev so replay sees the same inputs
10	// the worker saw at execution time.
11	
12	import (
13		"fmt"
14		"os"
15		"os/exec"
16		"path/filepath"
17		"strings"
18		"sync"
19		"testing"
20		"time"
21	)
22	
23	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
24	// Test helpers (real git repo fixtures)
25	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	
27	type landTestRepo struct {
28		t       *testing.T
29		dir     string
30		origin  string // path to a bare origin repo, or "" if no remote
31		branch  string // target branch
32		baseSHA string // the initial commit on the target branch
33	}

35	func newLandTestRepo(t *testing.T) *landTestRepo {
36		t.Helper()
37		dir := t.TempDir()
38		r := &landTestRepo{t: t, dir: dir, branch: "main"}
39		r.runGit("init", "-b", "main")
40		r.runGit("config", "user.name", "Test")
41		r.runGit("config", "user.email", "test@test.local")
42		r.writeFile("README.md", "# test\n")
43		r.runGit("add", "-A")
44		r.runGit("commit", "-m", "init")
45		r.baseSHA = r.resolveRef("refs/heads/main")
46		return r
47	}
48	
49	// newLandTestRepoWithOrigin creates a test repo whose origin is a separate
50	// bare repo. Used by the push-ff-only test.
51	func newLandTestRepoWithOrigin(t *testing.T) *landTestRepo {
52		t.Helper()
53		r := newLandTestRepo(t)
54	
55		bareDir := t.TempDir()
56		cmd := exec.Command("git", "init", "--bare", "-b", "main", bareDir)
57		if out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
58			t.Fatalf("git init --bare: %s: %v", string(out), err)
59		}
60		r.origin = bareDir
61		r.runGit("remote", "add", "origin", bareDir)
62		r.runGit("push", "-u", "origin", "main")
63		return r
64	}

66	func (r *landTestRepo) runGit(args ...string) string {
67		r.t.Helper()
68		cmd := exec.Command("git", append([]string{"-C", r.dir}, args...)...)
69		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
70		if err != nil {
71			r.t.Fatalf("git %s: %s: %v", strings.Join(args, " "), string(out), err)
72		}
73		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
74	}
75	
76	func (r *landTestRepo) writeFile(path, content string) {
77		r.t.Helper()
78		full := filepath.Join(r.dir, path)
79		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(full), 0o755); err != nil {
80			r.t.Fatal(err)
81		}
82		if err := os.WriteFile(full, []byte(content), 0o644); err != nil {
83			r.t.Fatal(err)
84		}
85	}

87	func (r *landTestRepo) resolveRef(ref string) string {
88		r.t.Helper()
89		return r.runGit("rev-parse", ref)
90	}
91	
92	// commitOn creates a detached-head commit at baseSHA in a throwaway worktree
93	// and returns the new commit SHA. The commit is reachable via object store
94	// but not via any branch in the main repo, simulating what a worker worktree
95	// produces after ExecuteBead cleans up.
96	func (r *landTestRepo) commitOn(baseSHA, path, content, msg string) string {
97		r.t.Helper()
98		wt, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "land-test-wt-*")
99		if err != nil {
100			r.t.Fatal(err)
101		}
102		_ = os.RemoveAll(wt)
103		r.runGit("worktree", "add", "--detach", wt, baseSHA)
104		defer func() {
105			r.runGit("worktree", "remove", "--force", wt)
106			_ = os.RemoveAll(wt)
107		}()
108	
109		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(wt, path), []byte(content), 0o644); err != nil {
110			r.t.Fatal(err)
111		}
112		cmd := exec.Command("git", "-C", wt, "add", "-A")
113		if out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
114			r.t.Fatalf("git add: %s: %v", string(out), err)
115		}
116		cmd = exec.Command("git", "-C", wt, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@test.local", "commit", "-m", msg)
117		if out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
118			r.t.Fatalf("git commit: %s: %v", string(out), err)
119		}
120		cmd = exec.Command("git", "-C", wt, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
121		out, err := cmd.Output()
122		if err != nil {
123			r.t.Fatalf("git rev-parse HEAD: %v", err)
124		}
125		sha := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
126	
127		// Pin the commit with a temporary ref so it survives the worktree
128		// cleanup. The test caller is responsible for Land()-ing it later.
129		ref := fmt.Sprintf("refs/ddx/test-pins/%s", sha[:12])
130		r.runGit("update-ref", ref, sha)
131		return sha
132	}
133	
134	// mergeCommitCount returns the number of merge commits (commits with >1
135	// parent) reachable from ref. Used to assert merge-commit semantics on the
136	// merge path.
137	func (r *landTestRepo) mergeCommitCount(ref string) int {
138		r.t.Helper()
139		out := r.runGit("log", "--merges", "--format=%H", ref)
140		if out == "" {
141			return 0
142		}
143		return len(strings.Split(out, "\n"))
144	}
145	
146	// commitCount returns the total number of commits reachable from ref.
147	func (r *landTestRepo) commitCount(ref string) int {
148		r.t.Helper()
149		out := r.runGit("rev-list", "--count", ref)
150		n := 0
151		_, _ = fmt.Sscanf(out, "%d", &n)
152		return n
153	}
154	
155	// commitParents returns the parent SHAs of sha.
156	func (r *landTestRepo) commitParents(sha string) []string {
157		r.t.Helper()
158		out := r.runGit("rev-list", "--parents", "-n", "1", sha)
159		fields := strings.Fields(out)
160		if len(fields) <= 1 {
161			return nil
162		}
163		return fields[1:]

1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to DDx are documented in this file.
4	
5	## [Unreleased]
6	
7	### Breaking changes
8	
9	#### Removed: `agent.routing.default_harness`
10	
11	The `agent.routing.default_harness` field has been removed from `.ddx/config.yaml`
12	(bead ddx-87fb72c2). DDx now refuses to load any config that still carries it
13	and prints a migration pointer instead.
14	
15	Why: `default_harness` was a silent fallback that competed with the top-level
16	`agent.harness` setting and the live route-resolution call graph. It produced
17	confusing routing decisions when the upstream service had no viable provider for
18	the requested profile. The endpoint-first redesign (epic ddx-fdd3ea36) makes
19	harness selection an explicit decision per dispatch — a config-level "default"
20	no longer fits.
21	
22	Migration: delete the field. The top-level `agent.harness` is still honored as
23	a tie-break preference on the resolution path (NOT a default override). See
24	[docs/migrations/routing-config.md](docs/migrations/routing-config.md).
25	
26	#### Opt-in: `agent.routing.profile_ladders` and `agent.routing.model_overrides`
27	
28	These fields are no longer consulted on the default execute path. They are now
29	explicit opt-in:
30	
31	- `profile_ladders` is consulted only when `--escalate` is passed.
32	- `model_overrides` is consulted only when the new `--override-model` flag is
33	  passed.
34	
35	Configs that still set these fields will load successfully but emit a one-time
36	process warning at config-load time. To silence the warning, either pass the
37	opt-in flag on the relevant invocation or remove the field. See the migration
38	guide for details.
39	
40	### Fix: `latency_ms` in claude harness traces now reflects per-call duration
41	
42	Previously every `llm.response` event in `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-claude-*.jsonl`
43	had `latency_ms == elapsed_ms` because the claude stream parser wrote the
44	cumulative wall-clock value into both fields. Per-call LLM latency was
45	therefore unrecoverable from captured traces.
46	
47	The parser now tracks the time the most recent LLM request was sent (session
48	start, then reset on each `user`/tool_result event) and emits
49	`latency_ms = now - lastRequestSentAt` for each assistant turn. `elapsed_ms`
50	remains cumulative, so for any event past turn 1 the two values diverge.
51	
52	Legacy traces produced before this commit have `latency_ms == elapsed_ms` and
53	should be ignored by latency-analysis tooling for runs predating the fix.
54	
55	Regression coverage: `TestParseClaudeStreamLatencyIsPerCall` in
56	`cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go` drives two assistant turns through
57	the parser with real sleeps and asserts (a) `latency_ms != elapsed_ms` for
58	turn 2, (b) the second-turn latency excludes the prior turn and tool window,
59	and (c) the sum of per-call latencies does not exceed the final event's
60	`elapsed_ms`.
61	
62	### execute-loop: structured `declined_needs_decomposition` outcome
63	
64	Adds a first-class outcome distinct from `no_changes` and `execution_failed`
65	for executors that conclude a bead is too large to deliver in one pass and
66	must first be split into sub-beads. Previously this case was conveyed via
67	free-form text in `no_changes_rationale`, and the loop kept re-attempting the
68	bead under a short cooldown — burning tokens to re-derive the same
69	recommendation across runs.
70	
71	The new contract:
72	
73	- `ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition` (`declined_needs_decomposition`)
74	  is a new value of the execute-bead status. Executors set it together with
75	  `DecompositionRecommendation []string` (recommended sub-bead titles) and
76	  optional `DecompositionRationale string` on `ExecuteBeadReport`.
77	- The execute-loop responds by appending a structured
78	  `decomposition-recommendation` event (JSON body carrying the rationale and
79	  recommended sub-beads) and parking the bead with a 365-day cooldown so
80	  subsequent loop iterations do not re-attempt it. The bead remains open;
81	  its status field is unchanged.
82	- A first-class CLI for the cooldown surface lands as `ddx bead cooldown
83	  show <id>` and `ddx bead cooldown clear <id>`. Operators should reach for
84	  these commands instead of editing the magic `execute-loop-retry-after`
85	  Extra key. The existing `ddx bead update --set/--unset
86	  execute-loop-retry-after=...` workflow continues to work as a power-user
87	  override but is deprecated for the decomposition case — clearing a
88	  cooldown via the new command is the intended path.
89	
90	Migration: this is an additive contract. Executors that do not emit the new
91	status see no behaviour change. Existing cooldown workflows (no_changes
92	short cooldown, push_failed long park) are unchanged.
93	
94	Regression coverage:
95	`TestExecuteBeadWorkerDeclinedNeedsDecompositionParksBead` in
96	`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go`.
97	
98	### Routing point release — ddx-agent v0.9.23
99	
100	DDx now consumes the cumulative routing contract delivered upstream across
101	`github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` v0.9.10..v0.9.24 (pinned at v0.9.23 in
102	`cli/go.mod`). Upstream shipped these capabilities incrementally across
103	multiple tags rather than as a single release, so this entry bundles the
104	combined contract DDx now relies on.
105	
106	Consumed upstream capabilities (commit hash · upstream bead):
107	
108	- **Cost-aware routing tiebreak** — `b85e90e` · [agent-0dafc7f0](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl) (regression coverage tightened in `9c80eb1`). Adds cost as a routing dimension so equally-capable candidates resolve to the cheaper provider.
109	- **First-class routing profiles** — `b645280` · [agent-191a74f9](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl). Ships the `default`, `local`, and `standard` profiles with zero-interaction defaults.
110	- **Routing reference docs** — `faafff2`, `db26108` · [agent-1f46cf22](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl). Publishes the routing reference and clarifies the best-provider cost baseline.
111	- **Public route decision trace** — `0bc2bf4` · [agent-53f38d95](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl). Exposes the candidate decision trace so callers can inspect why a route was chosen.
112	- **Supported-models allow-list** — `fd71e00` · [agent-bab52778](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl). Gates exact pins by the harness allow-list so unsupported models fail fast.
113	- **Typed explicit-pin errors** — `5992c7b` · [agent-dfabb10b](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl). Returns typed errors for explicit-pin routing failures instead of opaque strings.
114	
115	The combined contract DDx consumes:
116	
117	- **Zero-interaction defaults** via the `default` profile.
118	- **Profiles** (`default`, `local`, `standard`) selectable without bespoke config.
119	- **Override semantics** with explicit pins gated by the supported-models allow-list and surfaced through typed errors.
120	- **Public decision trace** for inspecting candidate selection.

input.new_string:
### execute-loop: recoverable push races and a 24h cap on loop-set cooldowns

Bead ddx-a458af7c. Two related fixes:

1. **Push-race auto-recovery.** When a worker's commits land locally but `git
   push` is rejected because origin advanced (the common multi-worker /
   multi-operator case), the land coordinator now attempts one
   fetch + merge + retry-push pass before giving up. Previously the bead
   was parked for a year and required manual operator intervention even
   though the work was already done locally and the merge was trivial. On a
   clean auto-merge the land returns with `PushRecovered=true` and the bead
   closes normally; the operator never sees the race.

2. **`push_conflict` is a distinct, structured outcome.** When the
   auto-recovery merge hits a real conflict (overlapping changes that the
   loop cannot resolve), Land() reports `PushConflict=true` and the loop
   maps it to a new `push_conflict` execute-bead status — distinct from the
   generic `push_failed` (and from `execution_failed`). The loop appends a
   `kind:push-conflict` bead event carrying the conflict context (detail,
   `base_rev`, `result_rev`, `session_id`) so an operator can find and
   resolve it without combing logs.

3. **Loop-set cooldowns cap at 24h.** Previously several paths
   (`push_failed`, `declined_needs_decomposition`, `review-manual-required`)
   parked beads for 365 days, which effectively meant "never retry." That
   should be a deliberate operator decision via `ddx bead update --set
   execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic loop output. A new
   constant `MaxLoopCooldown = 24h` and helper `capLoopCooldown` now clamp
   every `SetExecutionCooldown` call the loop makes. Operators extending a
   cooldown manually beyond 24h still works — the cap only governs
   loop-driven cooldowns.

   Note that this also changes the `declined_needs_decomposition` cooldown
   shipped earlier in this Unreleased section (the 365-day park described
   above); under the new cap, decomposition-declined beads re-surface within
   24h instead of being parked for a year. Operators clearing the cooldown
   via `ddx bead cooldown clear <id>` (the recommended path) is unchanged.

Regression coverage:

- `TestLand_PushAutoRecovery_RaceWithRemoteResolved` and
  `TestLand_PushAutoRecovery_UnresolvableConflictReportsPushConflict` in
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` exercise the new fetch +
  merge + retry-push recovery against real git repositories with
  side-clones advancing origin/main between local land and push.
- `TestExecuteBeadWorkerPushFailedCooldownCappedAt24h` and
  `TestExecuteBeadWorkerPushConflictParksAndEmitsEvent` in
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_push_failed_test.go` pin the 24h cap and
  the structured `push-conflict` event contract.
- `TestExecuteBeadWorkerDeclinedNeedsDecompositionParksBead` was updated to
  assert the new 24h cap (previously asserted >180d).

### execute-loop: structured `declined_needs_decomposition` outcome
